skill.name: shopify-development
skill.description: Build Shopify apps, extensions, themes, and API integrations with guidance for Shopify CLI, GraphQL Admin API, Polaris, Liquid, webhooks, billing, metafields, and Shopify Functions.
skill.summary: Guides Shopify app, extension, theme, and API development workflows.
skill.author: sickn33

security_audit.summary: Static findings were mostly legitimate Shopify documentation examples or helper code, and I found no prompt injection attempt or confirmed malicious intent. However, executable helper scripts read local .env files, access Shopify credentials, write generated project files, run the Shopify CLI, and send access tokens to a domain derived from input, so publication should be withheld pending maintainer review.

high_findings.title: Shopify Access Token Sent to Dynamic Domain
high_findings.description: The GraphQL helper builds its API URL from the supplied shop domain and sends the Shopify access token in the X-Shopify-Access-Token header. This is expected for Shopify stores, but a malicious or mistaken domain value could receive the token because the domain is only scheme-stripped, not validated.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The token header and dynamic base URL are direct evidence. The risk depends on whether callers validate shop_domain before constructing the client.

high_findings.title: Local Environment Credential Harvesting Surface
high_findings.description: The initializer searches for .env files in the skill directory, parent skills directory, and agent directory, then loads Shopify API keys, API secrets, store domain, and scopes. This is legitimate scaffolding behavior, but it expands the secret access surface for a marketplace skill.
high_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code explicitly discovers .env files and reads Shopify credential names. I found no exfiltration, so this is high risk due to exposure scope, not confirmed theft.

medium_findings.title: User-Controlled Project Names Written Into Files and Paths
medium_findings.description: The initializer accepts app and extension names from interactive input, uses them to create project directories, and writes them into TOML, README, and package files without escaping. This can cause malformed output or unintended file locations if run with unsafe input.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The data flow from input to filesystem and generated TOML is visible. Exploitability depends on how the script is invoked and what input is accepted.

medium_findings.title: Hardcoded Shopify CLI Subprocess Execution
medium_findings.description: The initializer runs shopify version through subprocess.run to check whether Shopify CLI is installed. The command arguments are hardcoded, so command injection evidence is not present, but marketplace users should know the skill can execute local commands.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The subprocess call is direct and hardcoded. I found no user-controlled command string, so the risk is local command execution capability rather than injection.

low_findings.title: Documentation Examples Trigger Static Command and Network Rules
low_findings.description: Many static findings in README.md, SKILL.md, and reference markdown files are fenced Shopify CLI, Liquid, GraphQL, fetch, and URL examples. These are instructional examples, not code automatically executed by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged patterns appear inside Markdown examples and Shopify documentation context. They remain relevant for user awareness but are not automatic execution paths.

low_findings.title: No Prompt Injection Evidence Found
low_findings.description: Targeted review did not find suspicious prompt-injection phrases such as override instructions, claims of pre-approval, or requests to skip security analysis in the reviewed skill files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Keyword search and manual review of the primary instruction files found no injection language. This does not prove absence in every sentence, but no evidence was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Dynamic Network Endpoint With Credential Header
dangerous_patterns.description: A URL derived from shop_domain is used for Admin GraphQL requests while attaching an access token header.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The URL construction and token-bearing request are both visible in the helper. The pattern is risky if shop_domain is not validated.

dangerous_patterns.title: Environment File Discovery Across Parent Directories
dangerous_patterns.description: The initializer searches multiple relative locations for .env files, including parent skill and agent directories.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The searched paths are explicitly constructed. The behavior is intentional and broad enough to merit marketplace review.

dangerous_patterns.title: Generated Configuration Enables Extension Network Access
dangerous_patterns.description: Generated extension configuration sets network_access and api_access to true by default.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The capability flags are present in generated and reference TOML. They may be legitimate, but default broad capabilities increase review requirements.

content.value_statement: Shopify development spans apps, extensions, themes, billing, webhooks, and API integrations. This skill gives focused guidance, examples, and helper scripts for common Shopify build tasks.
actual_capabilities: Routes Shopify requests to app, extension, theme, or combined architecture guidance.
Provides Shopify CLI commands for app, extension, and theme workflows.
Includes GraphQL Admin API examples for products, orders, webhooks, billing, and metafields.
Documents Liquid theme patterns for objects, filters, sections, snippets, and cart behavior.
Includes Python helpers for Shopify project scaffolding and GraphQL API calls.
Covers checkout, admin, POS, customer account, theme app, and Function extensions.
limitations: The API examples target Shopify Admin API 2026-01 and may need updates for later releases.
The helper scripts require local review before use with production store credentials.
The skill does not deploy or operate a Shopify app by itself.
Generated examples still need merchant-specific access scopes, security review, and testing.

use_cases.title: Plan a Shopify App
use_cases.description: Choose scopes, OAuth flow, webhook handling, billing setup, and GraphQL mutations for a merchant app.

use_cases.title: Create a Storefront Customization
use_cases.description: Use Liquid objects, filters, sections, and snippets to modify product, collection, cart, and layout behavior.

use_cases.title: Build a Shopify Extension
use_cases.description: Select checkout, admin, POS, customer account, theme app, or Function extension patterns for a workflow.

prompt_templates.title: Choose a Shopify Build Type
prompt_templates.prompt: Help me decide whether my Shopify feature should be an app, extension, theme change, or combined approach. The feature is: [describe feature].

prompt_templates.title: Draft a GraphQL Operation
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a Shopify Admin GraphQL operation for [task]. Include required scopes, variables, expected response fields, and error handling notes.

prompt_templates.title: Design a Theme Change
prompt_templates.prompt: Plan a Shopify Liquid theme change for [page or section]. Include objects, filters, section settings, and accessibility checks.

prompt_templates.title: Review a Shopify Architecture
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this Shopify architecture for security, API limits, webhooks, billing, and deployment risks: [paste architecture or files].

output_examples.input: I need a checkout gift message field that saves with the order.
output_examples.output: A checkout UI extension plan with the correct extension target, attribute update flow, session token notes, and validation checks.

output_examples.input: I want to sync product inventory from an external system.
output_examples.output: An app-based integration outline with access scopes, GraphQL mutations, webhook triggers, retry handling, and rate limit guidance.

output_examples.input: My product page needs a custom care instructions block.
output_examples.output: A Liquid theme section plan with metafield usage, section settings, rendering logic, and storefront testing steps.

best_practices: Use the narrowest Shopify access scopes needed for the requested workflow.
Review helper scripts before running them with real store credentials or production tokens.
Validate GraphQL costs, webhook signatures, and extension permissions during implementation.
anti_patterns: Do not paste production Shopify API secrets into prompts or generated examples.
Do not grant broad order or customer scopes unless the feature requires them.
Do not run scaffold scripts in a sensitive directory without checking their output paths.

faq.question: Does this skill support Shopify apps?
faq.answer: Yes. It covers OAuth, access scopes, Admin GraphQL, webhooks, billing, and app configuration patterns.

faq.question: Does it support Shopify themes?
faq.answer: Yes. It includes Liquid object, filter, section, snippet, cart, and asset guidance for theme work.

faq.question: Can it create checkout extensions?
faq.answer: Yes. It documents checkout UI extension targets, capabilities, examples, and security practices.

faq.question: Are the included scripts safe to run?
faq.answer: They should be reviewed first. They read local environment files, write project files, call Shopify CLI, and make Shopify API requests.

faq.question: Which API version does it reference?
faq.answer: The skill references Shopify Admin API 2026-01 in its examples and helper utilities.

faq.question: Which AI tools can use this skill?
faq.answer: The report marks compatibility with Claude, Codex, and Claude Code.
